plain_english_summary: Deterministic biosignal encoding product with ECG-backed proof, a Rust-backed core codec, and a Python package surface.
commercial_wedge: For medical-device firmware teams and clinical-data infrastructure teams that need deterministic, reproducible ECG encoding with auditable proof lineage. Business value: reproducibility and auditability for biosignal pipelines.
technical_wedge: Deterministic 8-primitive ECG codec with Python and Rust implementations, benchmarked on MIT-BIH, PTB-XL, EDB, and NSTDB. EEG and multimodal helpers remain auxiliary, not headline authority.
ideal_first_buyer: Medical-device firmware team or clinical-data infrastructure team evaluating deterministic ECG encoding with proof lineage.
deployment_model: Python package on PyPI (`pip install zpe-bio`), Rust crate (core/rust/), and embedded reference builds. Commercial posture: always in beta.
proof_anchors: validation/results/ — committed proof and readiness artifacts
ECG validation with deterministic round-trip fidelity
ZPE-IMC coordination artifacts for cross-repo compatibility
not_claimed: No claim that EEG helpers carry the same benchmark authority as the ECG path
No regulatory or FDA compliance claim
NaN-safe input handling not guaranteed — NaN inputs produce silently incorrect reconstruction (PRD=0.0 reported); input validation is the caller's responsibility
family_position: Independent biosignal product with ECG as the current authority surface; coordination artifacts with ZPE-IMC live under docs/family/.
